Hardware Projects

05

Self-initiated · Electronics class final

Truck Window Retrofit

Retrofitted the manual crank windows on my own truck to automatic power windows. Arduino controls relays and motor drivers; current sensing detects stall and reverses direction automatically. Designed, wired, and documented entirely on my own — also served as my electronics class final.

ArduinoEmbedded CKiCadRelaysMotor DriversCurrent Sensing
View full project →

$26k Yamaha Grant · Junior Capstone

Solar Boat

Secured $26k from Yamaha Motor Corporation to convert a pontoon boat into a solar-powered vessel. Led electrical system design, carpentry, and CAD across a cross-functional team. Presented at Davidson's Verna Miller Case Symposium.

Electrical SystemsFusion 360CarpentryProject Lead
View full project →

Senior Capstone · 50+ Member Team

Baja SAE — Suspension Lead

Lead suspension design and fabrication for Davidson's Baja SAE team. Custom components in Fusion 360 and SolidWorks, coordinating with local manufacturers, writing BOMs, negotiating sponsorships.

Fusion 360SolidWorksWeldingFabrication
View full project →

KiCad · Hand-soldered

Custom PCB — Reaction Time Game

Designed and fabricated a custom PCB from scratch using KiCad. Schematic to physical board, hand-soldered and fully functional.

KiCadPCB DesignArduino
Watch demo →

Self-built · Motion Control

CNC Pencil Writing Machine

Built a CNC machine from scratch — gantry system, motion control, mechanical design. No kit.

CNCMotion ControlFabrication
Watch demo →

Software & AI

Founder · Closing seed round

Campus Swap

Full-stack student marketplace for move-out consignment. Students drop off furniture at move-out, we sell it at move-in, they get a cut. Built with Flask/Python, live at UNC Chapel Hill. Currently closing a $100k seed round.

usecampusswap.com ↗
FlaskPythonFull-StackFounder
View full project →

AI Agents · 2026

AI Project Portfolio

A collection of AI and agent projects built while finishing senior year — Gmail Declutter Agent, bracketAnalyzer, Autogram, and more. Full portfolio with live GitHub links tracked at vibepm-six.vercel.app.

vibepm-six.vercel.app ↗

View full project →

About

Hi! I'm Henry Russell, a Physics major at Davidson College with a concentration in CS and Math. I'm drawn to the intersection of hardware and software, and I believe the next big thing in technology is AI integrated with physical systems. Not just AI in a browser tab, but AI that moves things, controls things, and keeps running when you walk away.

I get a specific kind of satisfaction from building systems that act on their own. Whether that's an agent running in the cloud or an electronics project that does something real at the press of a button, the moment it works without me is the moment I feel like it's done.

I've spent three years running Davidson's student makerspace, led multiple capstone engineering projects, and I'm always halfway through something new. I learn best by building things that are just a little harder than what I've built before.

Currently recruiting

Actively looking for full-time roles in robotics, autonomous systems, and hardware/AI integration. Available May 2026.
